{{Concept | Name=UConn Huskies | Guid=3015-13220 | Aliases=Connecticut Huskies | Deck=The Huskies are the University of Connecticut's athletics teams. | Characters= | Concepts= | Franchises= | Games=Games/EA_Sports_College_Football_25,Games/EA_Sports_College_Football_26,Games/March_Madness_98,Games/NCAA_Basketball_10 | Locations=Locations/Connecticut,Locations/New_England,Locations/Storrs,Locations/The_United_States_of_America | Objects= | People= | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/0/1992/3608653-8528261718-800px.png}}

{{Concept | Name=Urkador | Guid=3015-13265 | Deck=A ghoulish creature created for Ember Sword, urkadors are aggressive malformed monsters that commonly dwell within the subterranean depths of Thanabus. Socially pack-minded, urkadors rally around their uberdor leaders and rely upon numbers to overwhelm enemies. | Characters= | Concepts=Concepts/Ghoul | Franchises= | Games=Games/Ember_Sword | Locations=Locations/Solarwood,Locations/Thanabus | Objects= | People=People/Samuel_Horton | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/13/137364/3651547-urkador.png}}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"3651547-urkador.png","path":"13\/137364\/","mime":"image\/png","sizes":"scale_small,square_small,square_medium,scale_medium,square_tiny,square_avatar,scale_super,square_mini,scale_tiny,screen_kubrick,scale_avatar,scale_large,screen_medium","caption":null},"background":[]}' /> A ghoulish creature created for Ember Sword, urkadors are aggressive malformed monsters that commonly dwell within the subterranean depths of Thanabus. Socially pack-minded, urkadors rally around their uberdor leaders and rely upon numbers to overwhelm enemies.

{{Concept |Guid=3015-13618 |Name=Virtual Boy – Nintendo Classics |Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/0/1992/3797076-untitled-1.png |Deck=Virtual Boy classics made available to subscribers of Nintendo Switch Online Expansion Pack. |Concepts=Concepts/Game_Boy_Advance_Nintendo_Classics, Concepts/Game_Boy_Nintendo_Classics, Concepts/Nintendo_64_Nintendo_Classics, Concepts/Nintendo_Entertainment_System_Nintendo_Classics, Concepts/Nintendo_GameCube_Nintendo_Classics, Concepts/Nintendo_Switch_Online, Concepts/Sega_Genesis_Nintendo_Classics |Games=Games/3D_Tetris, Games/Galactic_Pinball, Games/Golf_1995, Games/Insmouse_no_Yakata, Games/Jack_Bros, Games/Mario_Clash, Games/Marios_Tennis, Games/Red_Alarm, Games/Space_Invaders_Virtual_Collection, Games/Teleroboxer, Games/V_Tetris, Games/Vertical_Force, Games/Virtual_Bowling, Games/Virtual_Boy_Wario_Land }} ==Overview== The [[Platforms/Virtual_Boy|Virtual Boy]] Switch Online subscription service will launch on February 17, 2026.
{| class='wikitable' style='margin:auto;width:100%;' |- ! Date||Game||NA/PAL/KR||JP/HK |- | February 17, 2026||[[Games/3D_Tetris|3D Tetris]]|||| |- | ||[[Games/Galactic_Pinball|Galactic Pinball]]|||| |- | ||[[Games/Golf_1995|Golf]]|||| |- | ||[[Games/Insmouse_no_Yakata|Insmouse no Yakata]]|||| |- | ||[[Games/Red_Alarm|Red Alarm]]|||| |- | ||[[Games/Teleroboxer|Teleroboxer]]|||| |- | ||[[Games/Virtual_Boy_Wario_Land|Virtual Boy Wario Land]]|||| |- | March 10, 2026||[[Games/Mario_Clash|Mario Clash]]|||| |- | ||[[Games/Marios_Tennis|Mario's Tennis]]|||| |- | May 14, 2026||[[Games/Jack_Bros|Jack Bros.]]|||| |- | ||[[Games/Space_Invaders_Virtual_Collection|Space Invaders Virtual Collection]]|||| |- | ||[[Games/V_Tetris|V-Tetris]]|||| |- | ||[[Games/Vertical_Force|Vertical Force]]|||| |- | ||[[Games/Virtual_Bowling|Virtual Bowling]]|||| |}

{{Concept | Name=Wearing Animal Skins | Guid=3015-13391 | Deck=It is common to make foot mats or neat coats from the skins of animals, but sometimes they are worn in their entirety, together with the scalp of the head, as a cape or scarf. A demonstration of closeness to nature or the predatory nature of one's nature | Characters=Characters/Chief_Thunder,Characters/Rock,Characters/Sun_Wukong | Concepts=Concepts/Barbarians,Concepts/Berserkers,Concepts/Feral_Human,Concepts/Followers_of_Romulus,Concepts/Fury,Concepts/Hunter_3838,Concepts/Hunting,Concepts/Lions,Concepts/Middle_Ages,Concepts/Poaching,Concepts/Skinning,Concepts/Wolf | Franchises= | Games=Games/Assassins_Creed_Brotherhood,Games/Conkers_Bad_Fur_Day,Games/Killer_Instinct_2013,Games/Leisure_Suit_Larry_Wet_Dreams_Dry_Twice,Games/Mortal_Kombat_2011,Games/Shadow_of_Rome,Games/Soul_Edge,Games/The_Operative_No_One_Lives_Forever | Locations=Locations/Canyon,Locations/Cave,Locations/Forest,Locations/The_Ancient_World,Locations/Treehouse_Village | Objects=Objects/Bear_Skin,Objects/Loincloth,Objects/Pelt | People= | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/173/1734046/3687407-shadowofrome.jpg}}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"3687407-shadowofrome.jpg","path":"173\/1734046\/","mime":"image\/jpeg","sizes":"screen_kubrick,square_medium,scale_super,square_avatar,square_small,scale_small,scale_medium,square_tiny,scale_tiny,square_mini,scale_avatar,screen_medium,scale_large","caption":"Shadow of Rome"},"background":[]}' /> It is common to make foot mats or neat coats from the skins of animals, but sometimes they are worn in their entirety, together with the scalp of the head, as a cape or scarf. A demonstration of closeness to nature or the predatory nature of one's nature

revision.text:
{{Concept | Name=White Tiger | Guid=3015-13302 | Deck=The white tiger, or bleached tiger, is a leucistic morph of the tiger. | Characters=Characters/Baihu,Characters/Byakko,Characters/Tommy_Oliver,Characters/White_Tiger,Characters/White_Tiger_33796 | Concepts=Concepts/Boss,Concepts/Boss_Fight,Concepts/Sabre_toothed_Cats,Concepts/Tigers | Franchises= | Games=Games/Army_of_Two_The_40th_Day,Games/Blood_Cross,Games/Dark_Savior,Games/Jewel_Master,Games/Quintessence_The_Blighted_Venom,Games/Sacred_2_Fallen_Angel,Games/Sacred_2_Ice_and_Blood,Games/Violence_Fight | Locations= | Objects=Objects/Mega_Tigerzord | People= | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/173/1734046/3634626-%D0%91%D1%83%D1%84%D0%B5%D1%80%D0%BE%D0%B1%D0%BC%D0%B5%D0%BD%D0%B0_10-07-2024_02.jpg}}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"3634626-\u0411\u0443\u0444\u0435\u0440\u043e\u0431\u043c\u0435\u043d\u0430_10-07-2024_02.jpg","path":"173\/1734046\/","mime":"image\/jpeg","sizes":"scale_medium,scale_tiny,square_small,square_mini,scale_small,square_medium,square_avatar,scale_super,square_tiny,screen_kubrick,scale_large,scale_avatar,screen_medium","caption":"Jewel Master (Genesis)"},"background":[]}' /> The white tiger, or bleached tiger, is a leucistic morph of the tiger.

revision.comment: Point cover at a rendition that actually serves
revision.text:
{{Concept | Name=Window Breaking Enemies | Guid=3015-13374 | Deck=Enemies that enter a building from the street by breaking the glass on a window, or from the building in this way jump outside or into an adjacent room. They leave behind a hole and scattered shards | Characters= | Concepts=Concepts/Breakable_Glass,Concepts/Breaking_Through_Windows,Concepts/Defenestration,Concepts/Destructible_Environment,Concepts/Enemies_Breaking_Down_Doors,Concepts/Foot_Clan,Concepts/Window_Enemies | Franchises= | Games=Games/Alone_in_the_Dark,Games/Blade_of_Darkness,Games/Castlevania_Rondo_of_Blood,Games/Clock_Tower_1995,Games/Condemned_Criminal_Origins,Games/Dino_Crisis,Games/Final_Fantasy_VIII,Games/Grabbed_by_the_Ghoulies,Games/Lady_Stalker_Kako_kara_no_Chousen,Games/MediEvil,Games/MediEvil_2019,Games/Ninja_Gaiden_2004,Games/Ninja_Gaiden_3_Razors_Edge,Games/Ninja_Gaiden_Black,Games/Ninja_Gaiden_II,Games/Ninja_Gaiden_Sigma_2,Games/Resident_Evil,Games/Resident_Evil_0,Games/Resident_Evil_2,Games/Resident_Evil_2002,Games/Resident_Evil_3_Nemesis,Games/Resident_Evil_4,Games/Resident_Evil_4_2023,Games/Resident_Evil_Code_Veronica,Games/Resident_Evil_Survivor,Games/Shadow_Man_2econd_Coming,Games/Silent_Hill,Games/Teenage_Mutant_Ninja_Turtles_1989,Games/The_Callisto_Protocol,Games/The_Suffering,Games/The_Suffering_Ties_That_Bind,Games/Vagrant_Story,Games/Vivisector_Beast_Within | Locations=Locations/Church,Locations/Haunted_House,Locations/Mansion,Locations/Raccoon_City,Locations/Raccoon_City_Police_Department,Locations/Spencer_Mansion | Objects=Objects/Building,Objects/Glass,Objects/Magnifying_Glass,Objects/Stained_Glass,Objects/Window | People= | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/173/1734046/3665896-%D0%91%D1%83%D1%84%D0%B5%D1%80%D0%BE%D0%B1%D0%BC%D0%B5%D0%BD%D0%B0_12-18-2024_01.jpg}}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"3665896-\u0411\u0443\u0444\u0435\u0440\u043e\u0431\u043c\u0435\u043d\u0430_12-18-2024_01.jpg","path":"173\/1734046\/","mime":"image\/jpeg","sizes":"scale_super,scale_small,scale_medium,square_medium,square_small,square_mini,square_avatar,square_tiny,scale_avatar,scale_large,screen_kubrick,screen_medium,scale_tiny","caption":"Resident Evil (1996) - Window Breaking Enemies"},"background":[]}' /> Most foes break the window with their own body like a battering ram, but if the foe has intelligence, they may use a heavy object or shot first. Sometimes they don't reach inside entirely, but only try to grab the hero or throw something dangerous inside. In a fantasy setting, the enemy can jump out of the window pane itself, for example, materializing in a pattern of colored stained glass. Window parts between rooms are also taken into account, such as transom windows and observation windows, such as those found in an interrogation room. Showcases, ceiling glazing, armored transparent walls between rooms, glazed doors.

{{Concept | Name=Wisconsin Badgers | Guid=3015-13235 | Deck=The Badgers are the University of Wisconsin's athletics teams. | Characters= | Concepts= | Franchises= | Games=Games/EA_Sports_College_Football_25,Games/EA_Sports_College_Football_26,Games/March_Madness_98,Games/NBA_2K16,Games/NCAA_Basketball_10,Games/NCAA_Football_2002 | Locations=Locations/Madison,Locations/The_United_States_of_America,Locations/Wisconsin | Objects= | People= | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/0/1992/3608671-6424389751-800px.png}}

{{Concept | Name=Woozle | Guid=3015-13636 | Characters= | Concepts= | Franchises= | Games=Games/Piglets_Big_Game | Locations= | Objects= | People= }}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":[],"background":[]}' />

{{Concept | Name=Worker-Owned Developers | Guid=3015-13482 | Deck=AKA Worker Cooperatives. Game Developers that are owned by their workers. | Characters= | Concepts= | Franchises= | Games=Games/Braziball,Games/Dead_Cells,Games/Depanneur_Nocturne,Games/Edgar_Bokbok_in_Boulzac,Games/Epicinium,Games/Gardenarium,Games/Gnog,Games/Goodbye_Volcano_High,Games/Lara_Croft_Go,Games/Revenant_Hill,Games/Ridiculous_Fishing_A_Tale_of_Redemption,Games/Sally_2025,Games/Skullgirls,Games/Skullgirls_2017,Games/Spirit_Swap_Lofi_Beats_to_Match_3_To,Games/Tonight_We_Riot,Games/Windblown,Games/Winding_Worlds,Games/Yrminsul,Games/Zombinoia | Locations= | Objects= | People= | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/14/143948/3758169-twinpines.svg.png}}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"3758169-twinpines.svg.png","path":"14\/143948\/","mime":"image\/png","sizes":"scale_medium,scale_small,scale_super,square_tiny,square_medium,square_small,square_avatar,square_mini,screen_medium,screen_kubrick,scale_avatar,scale_large","caption":null},"background":[]}' /> Much like Giant Bomb is as of May 2025, these are developers (and their games) where the workers at the company have part (likely equal) ownership of that company. While this model is better for everyone involved, it also has challenges. For example, the lack of ownership-worker dynamic makes staying profitable harder in a capitalistic society where exploiting your workers to keep profits high is both the norm outside the company and not an option for the company. Development studios that have a Worker Cooperative include: * [[Companies/KO_OP_Mode|KO-OP Mode]] * [[Companies/Motion_Twin|Motion Twin]] * [[Companies/Soft_Not_Weak|Soft Not Weak]] * [[Companies/Lucid_Tales|Lucid Tales]] * [[Companies/Pixel_Pushers_Union_512|Pixel Pushers Union 512]] * [[Companies/La_Poule_Noire|La Poule Noire]] * [[Companies/Future_Club|Future Club]] * [[Companies/Black_Flag_Studios|Black Flag Studios]] * [[Companies/The_Glory_Society|The Glory Society]] * [[Companies/A_Bunch_of_Hacks|A Bunch of Hacks]]

{{Concept | Name=Wugtrio | Guid=3015-13538 | Characters= | Concepts= | Franchises= | Games=Games/Pokemon_Go,Games/Pokemon_Scarlet_Violet | Locations= | Objects= | People= | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/13/135472/3777852-0961wugtrio.png}}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"3777852-0961wugtrio.png","path":"13\/135472\/","mime":"image\/png","sizes":"square_small,square_medium,scale_super,scale_medium,square_tiny,scale_small,screen_medium,screen_kubrick,scale_large,square_mini,square_avatar,scale_avatar","caption":null},"background":[]}' />

{{Concept | Name=Wyoming Cowboys | Guid=3015-13236 | Deck=The Cowboys are the University of Wyoming's athletics teams. | Characters= | Concepts= | Franchises= | Games=Games/EA_Sports_College_Football_25,Games/EA_Sports_College_Football_26,Games/March_Madness_98,Games/NCAA_Football_09,Games/NCAA_Football_14,Games/NCAA_Football_2002 | Locations=Locations/Laramie,Locations/The_United_States_of_America,Locations/Wyoming | Objects= | People= | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/0/1992/3608672-1626092300-i.png}}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"3608672-1626092300-i.png","path":"0\/1992\/","mime":"image\/png","sizes":"square_avatar,square_medium,square_small,scale_medium,scale_small,scale_super,square_tiny,screen_kubrick,square_mini,scale_tiny,scale_avatar,scale_large,screen_medium","caption":null},"background":[]}' /> The Cowboys are the University of Wyoming's athletics teams.

revision.comment: Point cover at a rendition that actually serves

revision.text:
{{Concept | Name=ZenGin engine | Guid=3015-13466 | Deck=1997, The three were working at Greenwood Entertainment and dream of their own game. One day three students of the university in Oldenburg present their engine. Later the four employees leave and found Piranha Bytes. They license the engine of the three students to realize their vision of a RPG and hire the three students, who later are called "mad scientists" internally. At some point, the engine gets the name ZenGin and is being improved throughout the development of Gothic, until game released in 2001. | Characters=Characters/The_Nameless_Hero | Concepts=Concepts/Aberrant_A_I_Behaviour,Concepts/Action_RPG,Concepts/Artificial_Intelligence,Concepts/Dialogue_Tree,Concepts/Genome_Engine,Concepts/Non_Player_Character,Concepts/Polygonal_3D,Concepts/Voice_Acting | Franchises= | Games=Games/Gothic,Games/Gothic_Classic_Khorinis_Saga,Games/Gothic_II,Games/The_Chronicles_Of_Myrtana_Archolos | Locations=Locations/Castle,Locations/City,Locations/Farm,Locations/Forest,Locations/Island,Locations/Mine,Locations/Shack,Locations/Shrine,Locations/Stairway,Locations/Swamp,Locations/Temple,Locations/Town,Locations/Volcanic_Terrain | Objects=Objects/Bow,Objects/Campfire,Objects/Corpse,Objects/Facial_Hair,Objects/Forge_4736,Objects/Frying_Pan,Objects/Grass,Objects/Lightning,Objects/Meat,Objects/Ore,Objects/Pelt,Objects/Pickaxe,Objects/Potion,Objects/Shield,Objects/Ship,Objects/Stinger_4153,Objects/Stone,Objects/Sword,Objects/Table,Objects/Throne,Objects/Trail,Objects/Tree | People= | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/20/201266/3596282-nsw.jpg}}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"3596282-nsw.jpg","path":"20\/201266\/","mime":"image\/jpeg","sizes":"scale_super,square_small,square_avatar,scale_large,square_medium,square_mini,screen_kubrick,scale_avatar,scale_medium,scale_small,screen_medium,scale_tiny,square_tiny","caption":null},"background":[]}' />
===='''[https://www.worldofelex.de/en/game/the-engine The Engine] - '''''Written and translated by Ravenhearth''====
Flashback, 1997. Alex Brüggemann, Stefan Nyul, Tom Putzki and Michael Hoge are working at Greenwood Entertainment and dream of developing their own game. One day three students of the university in Oldenburg show up - Ulf Wohlers, Dieter Hildebrandt and Bert Speckels - and present their engine. Later the four employees leave and found Piranha Bytes. They license the engine of the three students to realize their vision of a roleplaying game and hire the three students, who later are called "mad scientists" internally. At some point, the engine gets the name ZenGin and is being improved throughout the development of Gothic, until the game is released in 2001. Until then, the three programmers might have left Piranha Bytes, but the engine stayed and powers the impressive RPG. The valley of mines of Gothic could be explored completely without loading screens, only when entering the four big dungeons loading was necessary. The view range in this open game world was gigantic and brought many computers to their knees. Players who gazed upon the valley with the old camp in the middle and sharp mountains in the background for the first time let their jaw drop, because an RPG in 3D still was something new back in the day. Besides openness and view distance the vertical dimension also impressed, which hadn't existed in other games like this before. The game world wasn't simply big, but also unbelievable lively: A dynamic day and night cycle and weather with realistic thunderstorms breathed life into the world, shooting stars were visible at the night sky and from time to time the magic barrier, which separated the valley from the outside world, was rumbling above the heads of the prisoners. Characters were spending the day realistically, they slept, got up, ate, washed themselves, ran around and talked with each other. Furthermore the game impressed with natural looking environments, DirectX 7 integration with hardware transform and lighting and with a beautiful vegetation, which all contributed to immersion. Gothic might didn't became a success over night, maybe also because of the high hardware requirements, the bugs and the strange controls, but developed into a secret tip. Piranha Bytes began with the development of an action game, which was suspended in favor of an addon to Gothic, which itself was later replaced by Gothic 2. It continued the story of the nameless hero, let the player return to the valley of mines and fight against dragons. Gothic 2 was released after only 11 months of development, but the graphics got visibly improved. The models had a higher polycount, the textures were more detailed and the draw distance was higher. The number of objects had been increased and the vegetation got better, which impressed with trees weighing in the wind. The game used DirectX 8 for better lighting and the water was prettier as well. There was a dynamic day and night cycle again and the game world could be explored without any loading screens, except when switching to one of the other two regions. Especially impressive was the densively populated harbour town Khorinis with it's many critizens and traders, which could bring the processor to it's knees pretty easily though. Gothic 2 had problems with bugs at release as well, but they were mostly fixed and so the game became a hit pretty fast. Ten months later the addon Night of the Raven followed, which is considered to be one of the best RPG addons of all time and transformed Gothic 2 into an immortal classic. NotR brought players to Jahrkendar, a previously unreachable part of Khorinis, in which the valley of makers and a desert canyon can be found and pirates wreak havoc. The addon improved the graphics only slightly, for example with a shine effects for metallic objects like cannons.

{{Concept | Name=Zen-chan | Guid=3015-13254 | Aliases=Benzo,Bubble Buster | Deck=Also known as Benzo or Bubble Buster, it is a clockwork recurring enemy in the Bubble Bobble family of titles. | Characters= | Concepts=Concepts/Recurring_Minion | Franchises= | Games=Games/Bubble_Bobble,Games/Bubble_Bobble_Double_Shot,Games/Bubble_Bobble_Old_And_New,Games/Bubble_Bobble_Part_2,Games/Bubble_Bobble_Plus,Games/Bubble_Bobble_Revolution,Games/Bubble_Memories_The_Story_of_Bubble_Bobble_III,Games/Bubble_Symphony,Games/Bust_A_Move,Games/Bust_A_Move_3_DX,Games/Bust_a_Move_Deluxe,Games/Bust_a_Move_DS,Games/Bust_A_Move_Millennium,Games/Bust_A_Move_Plus,Games/Bust_A_Move_Universe,Games/Chackn_Pop,Games/Classic_Bubble_Bobble,Games/Parasol_Stars_The_Story_of_Bubble_Bobble_III,Games/Popn_Pop,Games/Puzzle_Bobble_2X_Bust_A_Move_2_Arcade_Edition_And_Puzzle_Bobble_3_Bust_A_Move_3_S_Tribute,Games/Puzzle_Bobble_DS,Games/Rainbow_Islands,Games/Rainbow_Islands_Revolution,Games/Space_Bust_A_Move,Games/Taito_Legends,Games/Taito_Legends_2,Games/Taito_Memories,Games/Taito_Memories_II_Gekan,Games/Taito_Memories_II_Joukan,Games/Taito_Memories_Joukan,Games/Taito_Memories_Pocket,Games/Taito_Milestones_3,Games/Taito_Variety_Pack | Locations= | Objects= | People= | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/16/164924/3612454-2626939857-latest.png}}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"3612454-2626939857-latest.png","path":"16\/164924\/","mime":"image\/png","sizes":"square_small,square_avatar,square_medium,scale_medium,scale_small,scale_super,square_mini,scale_avatar,square_tiny,scale_large,screen_kubrick,screen_medium","caption":null},"background":[]}' /> Also known as Benzo or Bubble Buster, it is a clockwork recurring enemy in the Bubble Bobble family of titles.

{{Concept | Name=Zudomon | Guid=3015-13626 | Deck=Zudomon is an Ultimate-level Digimon usually associated with the Gomamon/Ikkakumon line. | Characters=Characters/Joe_Kido | Concepts=Concepts/AncientMegatheriummon,Concepts/Clockmon,Concepts/Digimon,Concepts/Digivolution,Concepts/Frigimon,Concepts/Garurumon,Concepts/Gomamon,Concepts/IceDevimon,Concepts/Ikkakumon,Concepts/MetalGarurumon,Concepts/Mojyamon,Concepts/Neptunemon,Concepts/Sukamon | Franchises= | Games=Games/Digimon_Adventure,Games/Digimon_Battle,Games/Digimon_Digital_Card_Battle,Games/Digimon_Heroes,Games/Digimon_Links,Games/Digimon_Masters,Games/Digimon_ReArise,Games/Digimon_Rumble_Arena_2,Games/Digimon_Soul_Chaser,Games/Digimon_Story_Cyber_Sleuth,Games/Digimon_Story_Cyber_Sleuth_Complete_Edition,Games/Digimon_Story_Cyber_Sleuth_Hackers_Memory,Games/Digimon_Story_Lost_Evolution,Games/Digimon_Story_Super_Xros_Wars_Blue_Red,Games/Digimon_Story_Time_Stranger,Games/Digimon_Super_Rumble,Games/Digimon_Survive,Games/Digimon_World_2,Games/Digimon_World_3,Games/Digimon_World_Championship,Games/Digimon_World_DS,Games/Digimon_World_Dusk_Dawn,Games/Digimon_World_Next_Order,Games/Digimon_World_Re_Digitize,Games/Digimon_World_Re_Digitize_Decode | Locations=Locations/Cyberspace | Objects=Objects/Furs,Objects/Hammer | People= | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/16/164924/3814532-zudomon_29_b.png}}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"3814532-zudomon_29_b.png","path":"16\/164924\/","mime":"image\/png","sizes":"scale_super,scale_small,scale_medium,square_tiny,square_small,square_medium,scale_tiny,square_avatar,screen_medium,screen_kubrick,scale_large,square_mini,scale_avatar","caption":null},"background":[]}' /> Zudomon is an Ultimate-level Digimon usually associated with the Gomamon/Ikkakumon line.
